Aerospace Mar 25, 2026

NASA proposes new strategy for commercial space stations

NASA, concerned about slow development of commercial markets, is considering revamping its strategy for supporting the creation of commercial space stations to succeed the International Space Station. The post NASA proposes new strategy for commercial space stations appeared first on SpaceNews.

NASA's shift signals potential acceleration of ISS transition timeline, creating urgent opportunities for aerospace contractors to develop station capabilities.

See SpaceNews →

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

United Airlines eases long-haul travel for families with new economy beds

Long-haul flights for families may become a little easier in the future after United Airlines unveiled plans to… The post United Airlines eases long-haul travel for families with new economy beds appeared first on AeroTime.

United's economy bed product targets the underserved family segment on long-haul routes, potentially differentiating its economy offering and capturing premium leisure demand without full business-class pricing.

See AeroTime Hub →

Military Mar 25, 2026

DOT&E Says No Combat-Capable TR-3-Configured F-35s Were Delivered in FY2025

The latest Director, Operational Test & Evaluation report says no combat-capable F-35 TR-3 aircraft have been delivered to the U.S. military until September 2025 because of the truncated software. The recently released 2025 Director, Operational Test & Evaluation (DOT&E) annual report brought ano...

F-35 TR-3 software delays mean the U.S. military received zero combat-ready variants in FY2025, signaling persistent capability gaps in fighter modernization programs.

See The Aviationist →

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

Wizz Air to test inflight connectivity, digital cabin with Immfly and gateretail

Wizz Air has signed an agreement with Barcelona-based inflight digital services firm Immfly and its partner onboard retail… The post Wizz Air to test inflight connectivity, digital cabin with Immfly and gateretail appeared first on AeroTime.

Wizz Air's partnership with Immfly signals ULCC adoption of digital cabin technology to enhance ancillary revenue streams beyond traditional seat sales and baggage fees.

See AeroTime Hub →

SMS Declaration of Compliance

What the SMS Declaration of Compliance means for operators and what the FAA expects.

Operators must formally document their Safety Management System compliance to demonstrate accountability and risk management capabilities to regulators.

Russian drone incursions into NATO airspace demonstrate escalating cross-border aerial threats that may compel European nations to expand air defense investments and surveillance infrastructure.

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

5 Exciting Design Features Of The Airbus A321XLR's Cabin For Long-Haul Travel

Passengers on 'thinner' long-haul routes stand to benefit considerably from the A321XLR.

The A321XLR's cabin innovations address underserved thin-route markets where larger widebody aircraft economics don't justify service, enabling airlines to profitably expand long-haul networks.

Military Mar 25, 2026

Drone enters Latvian airspace from Russia, crashes near Kraslava

Latvia’s Air Force has identified a foreign unmanned aerial vehicle entering the country’s airspace from Russia before crashing… The post Drone enters Latvian airspace from Russia, crashes near Kraslava appeared first on AeroTime.

Airspace incursions by unmanned systems from hostile nations signal escalating security challenges that require enhanced detection and response protocols for border protection.

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

"Inexcusable": Why Delta’s CEO Just Stripped Congress Of Their VIP Perks

Lawmakers will have to queue alongside regular passengers.

Delta's removal of congressional perks signals airlines' willingness to challenge political relationships, potentially reshaping how carriers navigate regulatory and legislative leverage.

Safety Mar 25, 2026

The Evolution of Aviation SMS

From the Wright brothers to the 2024 FAA rule — how aviation safety evolved from reactive investigation to proactive risk management.

Modern SMS shifts aviation from fixing accidents after they occur to preventing them through systematic risk identification and mitigation before incidents happen.

Military Mar 25, 2026

How The Northrop Grumman’s B-2 Spirit Cockpit Layout Prioritizes Long-Duration Mission Endurance

A closer look at the ergonomics of the world's first fifth-generation bomber.

The B-2's cockpit design emphasizes crew comfort and workload reduction for 30+ hour missions, setting ergonomic standards that influenced subsequent military aircraft development.

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

Couches In Economy: United Airlines Brings Premium To All Cabins With New Long-Haul Perk

UA's new Relax Row is rolling out in the main cabin this year.

United's Relax Row strategy extends premium seating economics beyond traditional business class, potentially creating new revenue streams from middle-market passengers on long-haul routes.

Safety Mar 25, 2026

Kona Storms Flood Oʻahu

Back-to-back subtropical cyclones in March fueled destructive flash flooding on several of the Hawaiian Islands.

Climate volatility in Pacific island regions poses operational challenges for hub airports like Honolulu, requiring enhanced weather monitoring and contingency planning for flight operations.

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

How Much Does It Cost To Fly A Boeing 747-400 For 1 Hour?

The Boeing 747-400 is both more expensive and less expensive to operate than modern aircraft with fuel spikes adding to the cost range.

Fuel volatility creates unpredictable operating costs for legacy widebodies, making 747s competitive only during low-fuel-price periods compared to newer efficient aircraft.

From the Flight Deck – Frederick Municipal Airport, MD (FDK)

Frederick Municipal is a small general and corporate aviation airport located just east of the city of Frederick, Maryland. The traffic mix runs the spectrum from single engine training aircraft up th

Frederick Municipal exemplifies how regional airports serve dual roles supporting both flight training pipelines and corporate operations within constrained infrastructure.

Commercial Mar 24, 2026

Digitizing borders could free up space to reimagine #PaxEx

Border control and lost baggage are the two remaining “pain points” when it comes to creating seamless air passenger journeys. But smart technology deployment and closer cooperation between airlines, airports and regulators could help resolve these issues and free up enough space to transform the...

Automating border and baggage processes could reclaim terminal space currently dedicated to queues, enabling airports to redesign facilities for enhanced passenger amenities and commercial opportunities.

See Runway Girl Network →

Commercial Mar 24, 2026

How Much Does Pilot Training Cost Before A US Airline First Officer Reaches The Flight Deck?

A closer look at the roadmap to the cockpit.

Aspiring airline first officers typically invest $150,000-$250,000 and 1,500+ flight hours before their first commercial airline paycheck, creating a significant barrier to entry that shapes pilot workforce demographics.

See Simple Flying →

Military Mar 24, 2026

U.S. Considering Deployment of 82nd Airborne Division to the Middle East

Senior officials are reportedly considering the deployment of the Immediate Response Force, which includes a Battalion Combat Team of the 82nd Airborne Division, according to U.S. media. After increasing rumors regarding a deployment of U.S. Army troops being in progress, new reports now mention ...

Military airlift operations may face increased demand if the 82nd Airborne deploys, straining transport aircraft availability and logistics networks.

See The Aviationist →

Premium For Everyone: United Airlines Invests In 3 Aircraft Types To Transform Flying

Since 2019, the legacy carrier has added nearly 40% more premium seats.

United's multi-aircraft premium expansion strategy signals that legacy carriers view premium capacity growth as essential for revenue recovery and competitive positioning against low-cost rivals.

Eastern Iowa Airport (CID) is a medium-sized multi-use airport located south of Cedar Rapids, Iowa. Traffic includes basic training aircraft up through air carrier aircraft. Flight schools often use t

Eastern Iowa Airport's diverse traffic mix from trainers to air carriers creates a complex operational environment requiring coordinated airspace management across multiple user categories.

Aerospace Mar 24, 2026

NASA PC-12 Aircraft Makes Move to Support Flight Research Across Agency

A NASA Pilatus PC-12 aircraft will now be based at NASA’s Armstrong Flight Research Center in Edwards, California, in order to support flight research efforts across the agency. The PC-12 was acquired in 2022 by NASA’s Glenn Research Center in Cleveland for use in advanced technology development....

NASA's relocation of its PC-12 to Armstrong consolidates flight research capabilities and enables broader cross-agency coordination for advanced technology validation programs.

See NASA →

Commercial Mar 24, 2026

Delta suspends airport perks for members of US Congress as shutdown strains TSA

Delta Air Lines said on March 24, 2026, that it is temporarily suspending special services for members of… The post Delta suspends airport perks for members of US Congress as shutdown strains TSA appeared first on AeroTime.

Delta's suspension of congressional perks signals how government shutdowns directly impact airline operations and stakeholder relationships beyond typical passenger services.

See AeroTime Hub →

Commercial Mar 24, 2026

Air Transat Scraps New 12-Hour Longest Route Before A Single Flight Takes Off

The leisure operator was due to take off from Toronto to Ghana's capital on June 27. But the route has been pulled before the first departure...

Route cancellation signals fragile economics for ultra-long-haul leisure routes, particularly to emerging destinations with limited traffic volume and yield sustainability.

See Simple Flying →

Industry Mar 25, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Operational optimization across three integration levels—airline, airport, and flight—offers faster emissions gains than waiting for new aircraft technology alone.

See MDPI →

Business Aviation Mar 24, 2026

How Much Does It Cost To Upgrade To Lie-Flat Business Class On Transatlantic Flights?

Booking business class isn't the only way to access lie-flat transatlantic aviation. Most major transatlantic airlines offer upgrades.

Premium cabin upgrades from economy or premium economy provide a cost-effective alternative to full business-class fares for transatlantic travelers seeking lie-flat comfort.

See Simple Flying →

Aerospace Mar 24, 2026

Geopolitical shifts bring opportunities and headaches for satellite execs

A shifting geopolitical landscape is driving huge business opportunities for satcoms but also bringing new supply chain and regulatory challenges, industry executives said March 24 at the Satellite Conference in Washington, D.C. The post Geopolitical shifts bring opportunities and headaches for s...

Satellite operators face a paradox where geopolitical fragmentation creates lucrative market expansion but simultaneously complicates manufacturing and compliance across increasingly bifurcated regulatory regimes.

Aerospace Mar 24, 2026

Reminders of Where We’ve Been, Where We’re Going

Three Moon rocks are on display during a March 24, 2026, event where NASA announced a series of transformative agencywide initiatives designed to achieve the National Space Policy and advance American leadership in space. NASA leadership provided updates on mission priorities, including sending t...

NASA's public display of lunar samples signals renewed commitment to deep-space exploration infrastructure that will drive demand for advanced launch systems and aircraft supporting space operations.

Regulatory Mar 24, 2026

FAA Expands Swift 100R Engine STC

Amended STC broadens engine coverage for the unleaded avgas.

The expanded STC reduces certification barriers for aircraft manufacturers seeking to adopt unleaded avgas, accelerating the industry's transition away from leaded fuel across more airframe models.

Aerospace Mar 24, 2026

3 Ways Students Can Get Involved With Artemis

NASA’s Artemis program will establish a sustainable lunar presence, unlock new scientific discoveries, and develop technologies for spaceflight to Mars and beyond – and students can help shape this new era of space exploration. As America launches this new Golden Age of innovation and exploration...

Artemis involvement opportunities provide aerospace students direct exposure to deep-space mission architecture, Mars-enabling technologies, and lunar operations that will define career trajectories for the next decade.

Multi-factor accident causation at major hub airports demands systemic safety review beyond single-point failures.

General Aviation Mar 24, 2026

NASA Selects Finalists in Student Aircraft Maintenance Competition

Editor’s note: This advisory was updated on March 24, 2026, to update the finalists list. NASA has selected seven student teams as finalists in the 2026 Gateways to Blue Skies Competition, giving them the resources to help address a critical challenge for U.S. aviation: aircraft maintenance. Chal...

NASA's investment in student maintenance training addresses aviation's acute technician shortage by channeling future workforce development through competitive incentive programs.

Drones Mar 24, 2026

ResilienX Receives FAA Waiver for Remote BVLOS Operations

ResilienX, Inc., a leader in aviation safety and BVLOS drone networks, announced today that it

ResilienX's FAA waiver enables commercial drone operators to conduct beyond-visual-line-of-sight missions remotely, reducing operational constraints that previously limited scalability of autonomous drone delivery and industrial inspection services.

Drones Mar 24, 2026

When Wireless Networks Falter, Drones Can Provide Backup Connectivity, According to Stevens Researchers

Researchers at Stevens Institute of Technology have developed a drone-based system designed to provide temporary

Drone-based emergency connectivity systems could reduce response times in disaster zones by bypassing damaged terrestrial infrastructure, offering new resilience strategies for first responders.

Drones Mar 24, 2026

America’s new 911 drone is always ready

Drones responding to 911 calls are no longer experimental. Across the US, police departments are already using Drone as First Responder (DFR) programs to get eyes on emergencies faster than patrol units. In many cities, drones arrive in under two minutes, well before officers. But even with that ...

Sub-two-minute drone response times are reshaping emergency dispatch protocols by providing situational awareness before ground units arrive, reducing response uncertainty and officer safety risks.

See DroneDJ →

Aerospace Mar 24, 2026

NASA X-Ray Mission Gets Fresh Look at 2,000-Year-Old Supernova

NASA’s IXPE (Imaging X-ray Polarimetry Explorer) mission has taken a new observation of a supernova, RCW 86, helping fill in a fuller picture of what other telescopes have observed. When astronomers using NASA’s Chandra X-ray Observatory previously targeted RCW 86, they discovered that a large “c...

X-ray polarimetry data from IXPE reveals magnetic field structures in supernova remnants that inform understanding of cosmic particle acceleration relevant to space weather prediction.

See NASA →
